text.title: php-phpunit-testing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for php-phpunit-testing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Classify every test as Feature (HTTP-driven, uses RefreshDatabase) or Unit (single class, no framework boot) before writing it.
rule.rationale: Feature tests use the full Laravel app kernel; Unit tests should use plain PHPUnit TestCase. Mixing RefreshDatabase into Unit tests boots the database and slows pure logic tests by 10x.

rule.statement: Place Feature tests in tests/Feature/ and Unit tests in tests/Unit/; mirror the app/ directory structure.
rule.rationale: Mismatched paths break test suite filtering (--filter=Feature) and make coverage grouping unreliable.

rule.statement: Use assertJsonPath('data.email', $expected) not assertJsonStructure(['data' => ['email']]) for value assertions.
rule.rationale: assertJsonStructure verifies key existence only; it passes when the value is null, 0, or the wrong type. assertJsonPath checks the actual value.

rule.statement: One behavior per test method; name the method test_<state>_<action>_<expected>.
rule.rationale: test_index is ambiguous; test_listing_returns_paginated_results_when_authenticated is self-documenting in failure output without reading the test body.

rule.statement: Call all *::fake() (Queue::fake, Mail::fake, Event::fake, Bus::fake, Storage::fake) before the action under test; assertions go after.
rule.rationale: Fakes replace the real implementation only from the point of the call; calling fake() after the action means the real implementation ran and the fake captured nothing — silent false positive.

rule.statement: Build test data via factories with relationships: User::factory()->has(Order::factory()->count(3))->create().
rule.rationale: Manual new Model() chains break when factories gain defaults or global states; factory relationships stay in sync with factory definitions automatically.

rule.statement: Use actingAs($user) for authentication in feature tests; never use Auth::login() directly in tests.
rule.rationale: actingAs() short-circuits middleware correctly and works with both session and token guards; Auth::login() may bypass important middleware checks.

rule.statement: One behavior per test. Name describes the behavior, not the method (test_listing_returns_paginated_users, not test_index).
rule.rationale: Descriptive names in failures save debugging time and make the test intent clear without reading the body.

rule.statement: Prefer assertJsonPath('data.0.email', $expected) over assertJsonStructure([...]). Path checks values; structure only checks keys.
rule.rationale: assertJsonPath is the strongest assertion available for HTTP responses; it catches wrong values, nulls, and type mismatches.

rule.statement: Always call *::fake() before the action under test; assertions go after.
rule.rationale: Fakes must be set up before the code that uses them runs, or they capture nothing.

rule.statement: Use factories with relationships: User::factory()->has(Post::factory()->count(3))->create() over manual chaining.
rule.rationale: Factory relationships stay in sync with factory definitions automatically; manual chaining breaks when factories change.

rule.statement: Mark slow integration tests with PHPUnit groups (@group slow) and exclude from default CI.
rule.rationale: Slow tests block PRs; move them to a separate job that runs nightly on real database drivers to catch dialect-specific bugs.

rule.statement: Use actingAs($user) for auth, not manual Auth::login — it short-circuits middleware correctly.
rule.rationale: actingAs() is designed for testing and properly activates guard middleware; Auth::login may bypass checks.

rule.statement: Keep DB tests on SQLite :memory: for speed, but run nightly on the real DB driver to catch dialect-specific bugs.
rule.rationale: SQLite is fast locally but behaves differently from Postgres/MySQL; nightly real-DB runs catch edge cases early.
